fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective_deadline`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective_deadline`), so an offer checked at exactly the deadline is incorrectly treated as still valid.

spec.expr: now < effective_deadline__1 or (now >= effective_deadline__1 and __cbfl_result == default)

constraints.intent: At the boundary (now == effective_deadline), the function must return default, not value; the strict > comparison fails this.
Verify effective_deadline is correctly computed so the boundary check uses the right value.
When the else branch (return value) is taken, now must be strictly less than effective_deadline; equality should have gone to the then branch.
The function must never return the offer value when now is at or past the effective deadline.
